relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks the way of cleansing.
The one healed from scale-affliction begins the return.
The priest goes outside camp to see whether healing has come.
Living birds, cedar, scarlet, and hyssop are gathered.
One bird is offered over living water.
The living bird is dipped with wood, scarlet, and hyssop.
Sevenfold sprinkling declares clean, and the living bird flies free.
The cleansed one washes, shaves, and returns near the camp.
On the seventh day the whole body is prepared for return.
On the eighth day offerings, flour, and oil are brought.
The person being cleansed stands before YHWH.
The reparation lamb and oil are waved.
The guilt offering is most holy.
Ear, thumb, and toe are marked for restored hearing, doing, and walking.
Oil is poured into the priest’s palm.
Oil is sprinkled before YHWH seven times.
Oil touches the same ear, thumb, and toe over the blood/life.
Oil rests on the head, and covering is made.
The priest makes covering from uncleanness.
Offering and covering complete the cleansing.
If the person is poor, a smaller offering is accepted.
Birds are enough when the hand cannot reach more.
The accessible offerings come on the eighth day.
The lamb and oil are waved.
Blood/life marks ear, thumb, and toe.
Oil is poured into the priest’s palm.
Oil is sprinkled before YHWH.
Oil marks the same places of restored embodiment.
The remaining oil rests on the head.
One bird is offered according to what the hand can reach.
Covering is made for the one being cleansed.
This is the accessible Torah for one whose hand cannot reach.
YHWH speaks of afflicted houses.
Even a house in the land may show affliction.
The owner reports the mark without pretending certainty.
The house is emptied before inspection to protect belongings.
The priest examines the wall’s appearance.
The house is shut up seven days.
The priest returns to see if it spread.
Affected stones are removed.
The house is scraped and debris taken away.
New stones and plaster repair the house.
If the affliction returns, it is examined again.
Spreading return marks malignant affliction.
The house is dismantled when healing does not hold.
Entering the shut house brings temporary uncleanness.
Lying or eating there requires washing.
If it does not spread after repair, the house is clean.
Birds, cedar, scarlet, and hyssop cleanse the house.
One bird is offered over living water.
The house is sprinkled seven times.
Blood/life and living water mark the house’s cleansing.
The living bird flies free, and the house is clean.
This is instruction for scale-affliction and scall.
It includes garments and houses.
It includes swelling, scab, and bright spot.
It teaches the day of uncleanness and the day of cleanness.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 14 is reintegration: the priest goes outside camp, living water meets living bird, the healed body is marked back into hearing/doing/walking, and even houses can be remediated rather than instantly condemned.
